title: Gilia texana
intro: Gilia texana, also known as the Texas gilia or Texan gilia, is a perennial herbaceous plant native to the southwestern United States. The plant has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, providing relief from various health issues, including inflammation, fever, and respiratory problems, due to its antiseptic, anti-inflammatory, and expectorant properties. The bioactive constituents of Gilia texana include flavonoids, phenolic acids, and terpenoids, which are responsible for its medicinal effects. The most common herbal preparations made with Gilia texana include teas, infusions, and extracts, often used in traditional medicine to treat respiratory infections, coughs, and colds.
taxonomy: Gilia texana is classified as a species within the family Polemoniaceae. This family includes approximately 23 genera and 200 species of flowering plants. Gilia texana is part of the genus Gilia, which comprises around 200 species. The genus Gilia is often referred to as the "gilia" or "skyrocket" due to its tall, slender spikes of colorful flowers. As a member of this genus, Gilia texana is a type of flowering plant native to the southwestern United States and Mexico.

The plant's extracts have shown to possess antimicrobial properties, which may aid in wound healing and protection against infections.
